message:
fix(fcp-host): widen intermediate arithmetic to u128 to prevent overflow in rate calculations

The EWMA latency tracker in resilience.rs and the success/error rate
calculators in rollout.rs performed intermediate multiplications in u64
space. Under sustained high-sample-count workloads, the product of
current_millis * retained_per_mille (or successes * 10_000) could
silently overflow u64::MAX, producing incorrect rate metrics that would
cascade into bad rollout decisions.

Changes:
- resilience.rs: LatencyEwma::update() now widens alpha, retained, and
  sample values to u128 before multiplying, then narrows back with
  u64::try_from(...).unwrap_or(u64::MAX)
- resilience.rs: ratio_per_mille() similarly widens numerator and
  denominator to u128 before the multiply-then-divide pattern
- rollout.rs: success_rate_bps() and error_rate_bps() widen to u128
  before multiplying by the 10_000 basis-point factor

All existing tests pass unchanged because the fix only affects edge-case
magnitudes, but the correctness guarantee now covers the full u64 input
domain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
